instructions: RepoContext serves compact, explainable, deterministic context from a local repository index. Start with one repoctx.get_context call using detail='slices' and responseBudgetTokens=2000; use 'outline' to survey more files or 'paths' when only locations are needed. Escalate only for a concrete gap: search when a needed file is missing, outline when a needed symbol was not delivered, and related_files for dependency or impact questions. Echo returned evidence receipts through seen to suppress exactly those pointers, spans, or symbols. Use known path@hash only when you independently hold the entire file; never derive it from a slice or outline hash. After edits call get_changes and re-index when stale. Stop once no evidence needed for the task is missing. Processing is deterministic, offline, and local; successful calls may append aggregate counts to the local usage ledger.

tools.name: repoctx.get_changes
tools.description: Diff the working tree against the index: added/modified/deleted files plus the indexed files that import or test them. Use after editing to learn what is stale (then re-run 'repoctx index') instead of re-reading everything.

tools.name: repoctx.get_context
tools.description: Ranked, explained context bundle for a natural-language task, packed into a real-BPE token budget. detail='paths' returns pointers with exact full-read costs; 'outline' adds symbol skeletons; 'slices' embeds the matching source spans and can avoid a full-file read. Echo a 'receipt' from earlier evidence via 'seen' to skip exactly that pointer, span, or symbol; pass path@hash as 'known' only for files you hold in full. Prefer this over reading files.

task.description: A natural-language description of what you want to do.

top.description: Maximum number of new files (default 8); reused units never consume a slot.

responseBudgetTokens.description: Hard ceiling on the exact serialized response.

projectedReadBudgetTokens.description: Hard ceiling on full-file reads implied by delivered pointers.

known.description: Files whose FULL content you already hold, each as path@hash. Never derive this from a slice or outline.

seen.description: Receipts from evidence you already received; only those exact pointers, spans, or symbols are suppressed.

tools.name: repoctx.get_outline
tools.description: A file's skeleton: symbols with signatures, lines and doc summaries, plus its content hash and exact full-read token cost. Use the compact skeleton to decide whether (and which part of) a file is worth reading.

file.description: The file (repo-relative or absolute path) to outline.

tools.name: repoctx.get_related_files
tools.description: List the files related to a given file: its imports, dependents and linked tests. Returns a JSON document (schema_version, results[] with path, relation and reasons).

file.description: The file (repo-relative or absolute path) to find related files for.

tools.name: repoctx.search
tools.description: Full-text (BM25) search across the indexed repository. Returns a JSON document (schema_version, results[] with path, score, kind, lines and machine-readable reasons). Use for finding files or symbols by term.

symbols.description: Restrict the search to symbols (classes, functions, ...).
